↗
May 26, 2026
Merge cells in simple tables
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Finally! Merge cells in simple tables.
Create headers that span multiple columns or group related rows into structured layouts.
Give it a try: select multiple cells → open the cell menu → select
Merge cells
.
See the
announcement
on X →
May 13, 2026
3.5
3.5: Notion Developer Platform
Notion? For developers? Fair question. It’s true, we haven’t always been the most developer-focused platform. Today that changes.
Introducing: the
Notion Developer Platform.
Now you (and your coding agents) can write code to sync any data and build any agent tool, all running on our infrastructure. You can also bring your favorite agents into Notion (like Claude, Codex, Decagon, or ones you’ve built yourself). It turns Notion into one shared canvas with all your data, for your team and agents to work together.
Learn more →
Orchestrate all your team’s agents (Alpha)
Bring your favorite agents into Notion with the External Agents API, even the ones you built yourself. We’ve also partnered with Claude, Codex, Decagon, and more so they work out of the box. Now Notion is your orchestration layer: a Decagon ticket routes to your coding agent, which proposes a fix and loops in your team to approve. Anyone can work with agents in Notion, not just engineers.
Join the External Agents waitlist →
Notion is our AI layer because it’s where work is created or imagined—and we want our agents as close to the action as possible.
Dan Gilbert
CEO at Brainlabs
Sync
any
data source (Beta)
Sync any data source with an API into your Notion databases. No servers for your team to manage. Our new database sync is powered by Workers that run on our infrastructure (more on this below). Pull in tickets from Zendesk so agents can take a first pass on the fix. Sync customer data from Salesforce for agents to build detailed reports. Connect Strava and Spotify data to curate the perfect running playlist. Whatever context you need can now live in Notion.
Watch the demo →
Workers give us the tools to build deep integrations into Notion that simply couldn't exist before. We have a worker that runs every night that syncs and converts uneditable PDFs in our Google Drive into rich, fully editable pages in an organized Notion database. It unlocks this data for our team and agents - and saves us tons of time.
Sam Lambert
CEO at PlanetScale
Build custom tools for your agents (Beta)
Give your Custom Agents capabilities that Notion and MCP don’t cover on their own. Write your logic in code and deploy it as a Worker. It’s
deterministic
, so it’s more reliable than LLM reasoning, and a
fraction of the token cost
. Use them to generate assets, query internal data, or take action in any other app.
Read the docs →
I think of Notion Workers as infrastructure: they auto-populate, auto-update, and set up the systems I need.
Austin Tedesco
Head of Growth at Every
Trigger Notion workflows from anywhere (Beta)
Webhooks used to be a one-way street: Notion could trigger your other apps, but not the other way around. Now any app can trigger Notion directly. A Worker receives the webhook, runs your logic, and takes action in Notion or calls other APIs. Use it to close tasks when a PR merges, update your CRM when a subscription changes, or create an onboarding doc when an offer is signed.
Read the docs →
Meet your Notion Workers
Database sync, agent tools, and webhook triggers are all powered by a new primitive we’re calling Workers. Notion Workers are our hosted runtime for custom code, so you can extend Notion without running your own servers. You and your coding agent write the code, deploy it through the CLI, and run it in a secure sandbox. Workers are free to try during the beta period. Starting August 11 2026, Workers will run on Notion credits.
Read the docs →
Workers let us connect directly to other tools’ APIs and automate what used to be manual handoffs. Notion becomes the connective layer, and Workers fill in whatever gaps exist between your tools.
Brian Emerick
Technical Program Manager at Vercel
A Notion CLI, built for devs and coding agents
The Notion command-line interface (CLI), made specifically for developers and coding agents, is a new way to work with Notion programmatically. Use it to sign in to your workspace, read and take action in Notion, build and deploy Workers, and extend Notion however your team needs. To install, run curl -fsSL https://ntn.dev | bash.
Watch the demo →
Use your Notion Agents in any app (Alpha)
Soon, your Notion Agents won’t have to stay in Notion. With the Notion Agent SDK, you can embed an agent inside your other tools. Trigger a deal report from a button in your CRM. Answer repeat questions inside MS Teams or Discord with verified knowledge from your workspace. Or pull customer context into Amplitude, Hex, or any dashboard.
Join the Agent SDK waitlist →
Manage all connections from one tab
We’ve updated the Connections tab in workspace settings. Now, every connection lives in one place, so your team can see everything that’s available at a glance. It includes personal and workspace connections, personal access tokens for API authentication, and internal API connections. And each app shows every connection type in one listing. Go to
Settings
→
Connections
to check it out (or
click here
).
Agents “hall of fame”
Knowing what agents to build can be the hardest part, so we pulled together the best agents from companies like Ramp, Clay, and Vercel into one library. Each one comes with a checklist of exactly what you need (databases, pages, tools) and a starter prompt to copy/paste. Pick one and set it up in minutes.
Browse the collection →
More updates your devs will love
Markdown API:
ICYMI read and write Notion pages as Markdown. Built for the way agents already think.
Notion MCP:
Now works with Meeting Notes and block comments, plus creating and updating databases are 91% more token-efficient.
Notion API:
Any member can build connections (not just Workspace Owners). Plus workspace-scoped OAuth and personal access tokens.
See releases →
Developer Portal:
app.notion.com/developers
is now a dedicated portal for creating, managing, and listing your connections and tokens.
Developer Docs:
Rebuilt and streamlined for clarity with a built-in AI assistant to help you find what you need.
This is just the beginning for the
Notion Developer Platform
. Any data, any tool, any agent, all running on our infrastructure. We can’t wait to see what you build.
Keep the feedback coming!
Ivan
P.S. We announced all of this and more at Make with Notion: Developer Platform.
Watch the keynote →
P.P.S. Curious what teams are already building? See how
Every
,
Brainlabs
, and
Vercel
are using our developer platform in production.
P.P.P.S. One more thing. You can now merge cells in simple tables, just like a spreadsheet. We’re excited about this one too.
May 7, 2026
Plan Mode
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
When you ask your agent to make significant changes like rewriting pages or updating databases in bulk, results can drift off course if your intent is unclear. Plan Mode introduces a preliminary step where your agent asks clarifying questions and builds a detailed plan before acting. The result is fewer surprises and more confidence in complex, multi-step work.
Learn more about
Plan Mode
from the Help Center →
May 6, 2026
New Custom Agent Directory
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Custom Agents now have a dedicated place in your Library. Use the Custom Agent Directory to browse all your workspace’s agents, pin your favorites, and create new ones to automate your team’s busywork 24/7.
Get started from the sidebar:
Library
→
Agents
.
May 5, 2026
New Custom Agent controls for admins
Two months ago, we launched Custom Agents in beta. Since then, teams have created more than a million of them and honestly pushed them further than we expected!
One theme from admins was consistent:
as Custom Agents scaled, so did the need for more control and visibility.
Your feedback shaped what we built next, so here’s what's new.
Set guardrails before Custom Agents run
Managing who can create Custom Agents and how much each agent is allowed to spend make rollouts easier to manage:
Start by choosing who create agents, either individuals or user groups, then expand access over time as you build confidence. You can do this in
Settings
→
Notion AI
→
Agents
.
To keep spend predictable, set per-agent credit limits with agent creators. If you’re on Enterprise and planning a broader rollout, you can also set one workspace-level credit limit that applies to all new and existing agents. Both controls are in your usage dashboard at
Settings
→
Notion credits
.
Track every agent and spend across your workspace
The Notion credits dashboard shows total usage broken down by agent, plus spend trends, status, and recent activity.
If an agent is pacing higher than expected, you can disable it right away in
Settings
→
Notion AI
→
Agents
, then work with the creator to adjust it before turning it back on.
If you manage multiple workspaces, you’ll also see a credits dashboard in your
admin console
to track credit pacing and Custom Agent usage across your organization.
Scale agents with built-in guardrails
A few things happen automatically to keep rollouts predictable even at scale:
Get notified by email and in-app as your workspace approaches its credit limit, so you’ve time to plan ahead.
If credits run out, all agents pause until you add more, so you won’t get surprise bills.
If a new agent starts spending unusually fast, we’ll pause it and notify the creator to review.
Members can request a higher credit limit, but nothing goes through without admin approval.
May 4, 2026
A new home on mobile
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Navigating Notion on mobile just got easier with the new home tab. Now your home, chats, meetings, and inbox are just one swipe away.
See the
announcement
on X →
May 1, 2026
Add Custom Agents to private Slack channels
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Custom Agents can now read and reply in private Slack channels. Get started from your workspace settings under
Settings
→
Notion AI
→
AI connectors
→
Enable access to private content
. Custom Agents only see the private channels you invite them to.
Learn more about our
Slack AI Connector
in our Help Center →
April 27, 2026
Rollups now as currency, percent, and more
Rollups now support number formatting (USD, EUR, percent, etc.) and decimal places. Show budget totals, conversion rates, and more in your rollups without any formula workarounds.
Learn more about
Relations & rollups
in our Help Center →
April 24, 2026
Find pages faster from the breadcrumb
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Hover over any item in the breadcrumb to see sibling pages, including pages inside databases. This makes it easier to find related pages and navigate your workspace.
See the
announcement
on X →
April 17, 2026
Mail & Calendar, now in your settings
Connect your calendar and inbox from a new dedicated tab in your settings. After linking accounts, Notion AI can handle work across your apps like scheduling meetings and drafting emails.
Next page

hits.title: Docs – Open source alternative to Notion or Outline
Notion – All-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, wikis, and databases
Focalboard – a self-hosted alternative to Trello, Notion, and Asana
Show HN: I am building an open-source Confluence and Notion alternative
Ask HN: Notion is withholding my company data, what can I do?
Lessons learned from sharding Postgres at Notion

analysis.name: Notion
analysis.tagline: The AI workspace that works for you.
analysis.value_proposition: An all-in-one AI workspace combining docs, databases, projects, and autonomous agents so teams can replace multiple point tools with a single platform.
analysis.ideal_customer: Knowledge-work teams (eng, product, marketing, design) at growth-stage to enterprise companies seeking a unified workspace with native AI automation

features.name: docs
features.description: Rich-text page editor with modular blocks (paragraphs, checklists, code, embeds, etc.)
features.evidence_quote: Docs – Simple and powerful.

features.name: blocks
features.description: Modular content units (to-do checkboxes, paragraphs, bullet points, etc.) that compose every page
features.evidence_quote: Blocks are pieces of content you add to a page (e.g. to-do checkboxes, paragraphs, bullet points, etc.).

features.name: unlimited collaborative blocks
features.description: No block cap for team collaboration; Free plan has a block limit
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited collaborative blocks

features.name: databases
features.description: Structured databases with subtasks, dependencies, custom properties, and multiple views
features.evidence_quote: Organize hundreds or thousands of items in one place, like projects or tasks – Databases including subtasks, dependencies, custom properties and more

features.name: database views
features.description: Multiple view types for databases (table, board, calendar, gallery, list, timeline)
features.evidence_quote: Database views

features.name: subtasks
features.description: Nested subtasks within database items for hierarchical project tracking
features.evidence_quote: Databases including subtasks, dependencies, custom properties and more

features.name: dependencies
features.description: Task dependency relationships between database items
features.evidence_quote: Databases including subtasks, dependencies, custom properties and more

features.name: custom properties
features.description: User-defined database columns with various property types
features.evidence_quote: Databases including subtasks, dependencies, custom properties and more

features.name: charts
features.description: Data visualization for databases: donut, bar, and line chart types
features.evidence_quote: Visualize data from databases in different ways (e.g. donut, bar, and line charts).

features.name: projects
features.description: Project management layer on top of databases for tracking work and progress
features.evidence_quote: Projects – Less tracking. More progress.

features.name: knowledge base
features.description: Centralized team wiki and single source of truth for documentation
features.evidence_quote: Knowledge Base – One source of truth for teams and agents.

features.name: notion ai
features.description: Built-in AI writing assistant for generating docs, summarizing, editing, and autofilling databases
features.evidence_quote: Notion AI – AI tools for work

features.name: ai trial
features.description: Limited trial of Notion AI capabilities on the Free plan
features.evidence_quote: Trial AI capabilities like generating docs, or autofilling databases

features.name: notion agent
features.description: On-demand AI agent that completes complex multi-step tasks using Notion context, connected apps, and the web
features.evidence_quote: Does work for you. Completes complex, multi-step tasks using context from Notion, your connected apps, and the web.

features.name: custom agents
features.description: User-created autonomous AI agents that automate repetitive team workflows (Q&A, task routing, reporting)
features.evidence_quote: Custom Agents – Automate repetitive work for your team.

features.name: q&a agents
features.description: AI agents that answer questions instantly using existing workspace knowledge
features.evidence_quote: Q&A agents – Answers questions instantly using knowledge you already have.

features.name: task routing agents
features.description: AI agents that autonomously assign, prioritize, and route tasks
features.evidence_quote: Task routing agents – Assigns, prioritizes, and routes tasks on its own.

features.name: reporting agents
features.description: AI agents that summarize, write, and send reports automatically
features.evidence_quote: Reporting agents – Summarizes, writes, and sends reports for you.

features.name: ai meeting notes
features.description: Automated meeting transcription and follow-up notes generation, no bot required
features.evidence_quote: Automate your meeting notes and follow-ups, no bot needed

features.name: enterprise search
features.description: AI-powered cross-app search across Slack, GitHub, and other connected tools (Beta on Business)
features.evidence_quote: Search across connected apps like Slack, Github & more

features.name: notion calendar
features.description: Calendar app that connects multiple calendars and manages time alongside Notion tasks
features.evidence_quote: Connect multiple Calendars to manage work and time together.

features.name: notion mail
features.description: AI-powered email inbox that syncs with Gmail for a personalized, intelligent inbox experience
features.evidence_quote: Connect your Gmail for an AI-powered, personalized inbox.

features.name: notion sites
features.description: Publish Notion pages as public websites with custom themes, favicons, and Google Analytics
features.evidence_quote: Customize your top header, choose a light or dark theme, add a custom favicon, and integrate with Google Analytics.

features.name: basic sites
features.description: Basic publish-to-web capability for Notion pages on the Free plan
features.evidence_quote: Publish a Notion page to the web – Basic sites

features.name: custom sites
features.description: Custom branding for published sites including header, dark/light theme, favicon, and analytics on paid plans
features.evidence_quote: Customize your top header, choose a light or dark theme, add a custom favicon, and integrate with Google Analytics.

features.name: forms
features.description: Capture and organize form responses inside Notion databases
features.evidence_quote: Capture, organize, and act on responses right inside Notion.

features.name: basic forms
features.description: Basic form creation available on the Free plan with Notion branding
features.evidence_quote: Basic forms

features.name: custom forms
features.description: Forms without Notion branding, available on Plus and above
features.evidence_quote: Remove Notion branding from public forms – Custom forms

features.name: automations
features.description: Built-in workflow automation triggered by database events
features.evidence_quote: Automation & connections – Automations

features.name: connections
features.description: Third-party app integrations that sync data and automate workflows with external tools
features.evidence_quote: Extend Notion with connections that sync your data, automate workflows, and connect your favorite tools.

features.name: basic connections
features.description: Core integrations including Slack and Google Drive on Free/Plus plans
features.evidence_quote: Connect to some tools like Slack & Google Drive

features.name: premium connections
features.description: Advanced integrations including GitHub and Asana on Business plan
features.evidence_quote: Connect to GitHub, Asana & more

features.name: advanced connections
features.description: Full integration suite including SIEM and DLP tools on Enterprise plan
features.evidence_quote: Connect to even more tools – Advanced connections

features.name: saml sso
features.description: SAML-based single sign-on for managed employee access at scale
features.evidence_quote: Manage employee access at scale with secure single sign-on.

features.name: granular database permissions
features.description: Row-level access control limiting collaborators to rows where they are assigned
features.evidence_quote: Collaborate in Notion databases without giving access to the entire database. Limit access to rows where the collaborator is assigned.

features.name: page verification
features.description: Verified badge on pages that are up to date, surfaced in search and AI citations
features.evidence_quote: Add a verified badge to pages that are up to date. Appears in search results and AI citations.

features.name: private teamspaces
features.description: Hidden teamspaces invisible to non-members for sensitive content like company planning or performance reviews
features.evidence_quote: Create teamspaces that can't be seen or discovered by anyone except those you add.

features.name: domain verification
features.description: Verify ownership of a company email domain to unlock SSO and advanced security features
features.evidence_quote: Verify ownership of an email domain to access advanced security features including single-sign on.

features.name: user provisioning (scim)
features.description: SCIM API for automated user and group provisioning and management
features.evidence_quote: Access the Notion SCIM API to provision and manage users and groups.

features.name: audit log
features.description: Detailed log of security and safety-related workspace activity for investigation and troubleshooting
features.evidence_quote: Access a detailed log of security and safety-related activity for your workspace account.

features.name: advanced security controls
features.description: Workspace-level rules preventing external sharing, disabling guests, restricting permissions
features.evidence_quote: Unlock extra permission controls to prevent certain people from sharing pages externally, disable guests.

features.name: zero data retention with llm providers
features.description: LLM providers use zero data retention for Enterprise workspaces when using Notion AI
features.evidence_quote: When using Notion AI, our LLM providers utilize zero data retention for Enterprise plan workspaces

features.name: dlp/siem connections
features.description: Integrations with DLP and SIEM tools to centralize audit logs and detect sensitive content
features.evidence_quote: Connect to SIEM and DLP tools to automatically centralize audit logs & set policies to detect sensitive content.

features.name: domain management
features.description: Claim company domain ownership and manage workspaces under that domain
features.evidence_quote: Establish ownership of your company domain and take actions on workspaces that belong to your verified domain.

features.name: customer success manager
features.description: Dedicated CSM to help Enterprise customers maximize Notion adoption
features.evidence_quote: Our team of Customer Success Managers are experts to help you get the most out of Notion.

features.name: teamspaces
features.description: Team-level organizational units within a workspace for grouping pages and members
features.evidence_quote: Workspace basics

features.name: sharing & permissions
features.description: Granular page and workspace sharing controls with guest support
features.evidence_quote: Sharing & permissions

features.name: comments & reminders
features.description: Inline comments on pages and blocks plus reminder notifications for collaboration
features.evidence_quote: Comments & reminders – Collaborate with others using comments and reminders.

features.name: import & export
features.description: Import from external tools and export Notion data in various formats
features.evidence_quote: Import & export your data

features.name: templates
features.description: Pre-built page and database templates for common workflows (32K+ life templates, 23K+ work templates)
features.evidence_quote: 32317 templates – Life / 23175 templates – Work

features.name: marketplace
features.description: Public marketplace for templates, agents, consultants, and integrations
features.evidence_quote: Marketplace – Templates, Agents, Consultants, Connections

features.name: web clipper
features.description: Browser extension to clip web content directly into Notion
features.evidence_quote: Web Clipper

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android apps for Notion
features.evidence_quote: iOS & Android

features.name: desktop apps
features.description: Native Mac and Windows desktop applications
features.evidence_quote: Mac & Windows

features.name: workers
features.description: Hosted serverless code runtime for custom integrations, agent tools, database syncs, and webhook handlers
features.evidence_quote: Notion Workers are our hosted runtime for custom code, so you can extend Notion without running your own servers.

features.name: external agents api
features.description: API to bring external AI agents (Claude, Codex, Decagon, custom) into Notion as orchestration layer
features.evidence_quote: Bring your favorite agents into Notion with the External Agents API, even the ones you built yourself.

features.name: database sync
features.description: Sync any external API data source into Notion databases with no server management required
features.evidence_quote: Sync any data source with an API into your Notion databases. No servers for your team to manage.

features.name: webhook triggers
features.description: Receive webhooks from any app to trigger Notion workflows and Workers
features.evidence_quote: Now any app can trigger Notion directly. A Worker receives the webhook, runs your logic, and takes action in Notion.

features.name: notion cli
features.description: Command-line interface for developers and coding agents to read, act, build, and deploy Workers
features.evidence_quote: A Notion command-line interface (CLI), made specifically for developers and coding agents.

features.name: notion agent sdk
features.description: SDK to embed Notion Agents inside other tools (CRM buttons, MS Teams, Discord)
features.evidence_quote: With the Notion Agent SDK, you can embed an agent inside your other tools.

features.name: public api
features.description: REST API for programmatic access to Notion workspace data
features.evidence_quote: Public API

features.name: embed support
features.description: Embed content from external tools (Loom, Figma, Mixpanel, Tableau, etc.) directly in pages
features.evidence_quote: View Loom videos directly in Notion

features.name: ai connectors
features.description: AI-powered search connectors for Salesforce, Jira, Linear, MS Teams, Outlook, GitHub
features.evidence_quote: Notion AI Connector for Salesforce – Search Salesforce accounts, leads, opportunities, and contacts in Notion

features.name: scim / sso identity provider integrations
features.description: User provisioning integrations with Rippling, OneLogin, Microsoft Entra ID
features.evidence_quote: Provision users & groups in your Notion workspace with OneLogin

features.name: siem integrations
features.description: Security log integrations with Splunk, Sumo Logic, Panther, RunReveal, Datadog
features.evidence_quote: Monitor and analyze your Notion logs from Splunk

features.name: dlp integrations
features.description: Data loss prevention integrations with Nightfall AI and Polymer for sensitive data protection
features.evidence_quote: Scan, monitor, and protect your sensitive Notion data to ensure continuous security and compliance

features.name: notion academy
features.description: Learning platform with courses, badges, and certifications for Notion skills
features.evidence_quote: Turn curiosity into capability — learn Notion, build smarter workflows, and validate your skills with badges & certifications.

features.name: notion credits
features.description: Usage-based credit system for AI agents and Workers consumption
features.evidence_quote: Free to try, then $10 per 1,000 monthly Notion credits.

features.name: database page-level access
features.description: Assign specific access levels to people mentioned in person properties in a database
features.evidence_quote: Database page-level access – Assign specific access levels to people mentioned in person properties in your database.

features.name: developer platform

features.evidence_quote: Introducing: the Notion Developer Platform. Now you (and your coding agents) can write code to sync any data and build any agent tool.

differentiators: Trusted by 98% of the Forbes Cloud 100
Over 50% of YC companies use Notion
Agents run 24/7 autonomously while you sleep
All-in-one platform replacing AI search, AI chatbot, meeting notes, writing assistant, email, calendar, project management, wiki, forms, and CRM
Hosted Workers runtime — extend Notion without managing your own servers
External Agents API turns Notion into an orchestration layer for all team and external agents
Developer platform with CLI built specifically for developers and coding agents

recent_ships.what: Merge cells in simple tables — create multi-column headers and grouped row layouts
recent_ships.source: Finally! Merge cells in simple tables. Create headers that span multiple columns or group related rows.

recent_ships.what: Notion Developer Platform (v3.5) — External Agents API, database sync, Workers hosted runtime, Notion CLI, webhook triggers, Notion Agent SDK
recent_ships.source: Introducing: the Notion Developer Platform. Now you (and your coding agents) can write code to sync any data and build any agent tool, all running on our infrastructure.

recent_ships.what: External Agents API (Alpha) — bring Claude, Codex, Decagon, and custom agents into Notion as orchestration layer
recent_ships.source: Bring your favorite agents into Notion with the External Agents API, even the ones you built yourself.

recent_ships.what: Database sync (Beta) — sync any external API data source into Notion databases via Workers
recent_ships.source: Sync any data source with an API into your Notion databases. No servers for your team to manage.

recent_ships.what: Webhook triggers (Beta) — any app can now trigger Notion workflows via Workers
recent_ships.source: Webhooks used to be a one-way street: Notion could trigger your other apps, but not the other way around. Now any app can trigger Notion directly.

recent_ships.what: Notion CLI — command-line interface for developers and coding agents
recent_ships.source: The Notion command-line interface (CLI), made specifically for developers and coding agents.

recent_ships.what: Notion Agent SDK (Alpha) — embed Notion Agents inside other tools like CRM, MS Teams, Discord
recent_ships.source: With the Notion Agent SDK, you can embed an agent inside your other tools.

praised: Unified platform value: 'There's power in a single platform where you can do all your work out of. Notion is that single place.'
Workflow efficiency: 'Streamlined workflows to reduce timelines by 3x.'
AI-native advantage: 'Using the most AI-native tools like Notion is an important competitive advantage for us.'
Speed of agent creation: 'Agents get created in three minutes between meetings, then hours of manual operational work disappear.'
Single-tool consolidation: 'Notion understands that you can solve a lot of problems with one tool.'
Workflow impact: 'Notion has been the most powerful and impactful way to streamline our workflow.'
complaints: Data portability: 'Ask HN: Notion is withholding my company data, what can I do?' (479 upvotes) — indicates significant concerns about data lock-in and export access
Not historically developer-friendly: 'Notion? For developers? Fair question. It's true, we haven't always been the most developer-focused platform.' (Notion's own admission)
Significant interest in self-hosted alternatives: multiple high-upvote HN threads about open-source Notion alternatives (Docs, Focalboard, Outline)
Performance/scale challenges implied by engineering post: 'Lessons learned from sharding Postgres at Notion'

free_tier.limits: Limited collaborative blocks; trial AI only; basic forms and sites; no custom branding

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Trial AI, basic forms, basic sites, Notion Calendar, Notion Mail, databases with subtasks

tiers.name: Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited blocks, unlimited file uploads, unlimited charts, custom forms/sites, basic connections

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: Notion Agent, AI Meeting Notes, Enterprise Search, SAML SSO, granular DB permissions, private teamspaces, premium connections

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Zero LLM data retention, SCIM, audit log, DLP/SIEM connections, domain management, customer success manager

pricing.notes: Custom Agents billed at $10 per 1,000 monthly Notion credits after free trial; Workers (Beta) free until August 11 2026 then credit-based; yearly billing saves up to 20% vs monthly
